revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:船橋カントリークラブ]]
'''Funabashi Country Club''' (船橋カントリークラブ) is a golf course located in Shiroi City, Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
== Overview == In the early 1950s, Shiroi Village in Inba District, Chiba Prefecture, was known for the Kioroshi Highway that ran through it, previously used by tanks and nicknamed the "Miscarriage Road."<ref name="funabashi">『Beautiful Golf Courses of Japan』, Golf Digest, 2013.</ref> The area featured vast woodlands, but few pathways.<ref name="funabashi" /> In 1959, during Japan's post-war golf course construction boom, Keiyo Tourism Development Co., Ltd. was established, led by President Takeshi Suzuki and Chairman Giichi Asai.<ref name="funabashi" />
Land acquisition in the Kiyoto area of Shiroi Village proceeded smoothly, and the course design was entrusted to Seizo Tomizawa, a former greenkeeper of nearby courses.<ref name="funabashi" /> In 1961, the company's name changed to Funabashi Country Club Co., Ltd., and construction began on an 18-hole course.<ref name="funabashi" /> The course opened on April 21, 1962.<ref name="funabashi" /> Featuring Bentgrass and Korai grass greens, it was later expanded to 36 holes.<ref name="funabashi" />
In 1969, a new 18-hole course opened south of the original. However, the land was later repurposed for Chiba New Town and the Hokusō Line, leading to the closure of the new course in 1983.<ref name="funabashi" />
== Location == 703 Kiyoto, Shiroi City, Chiba Prefecture, 270-1415, Japan
== Course Information == * '''Opening Date:''' April 20, 1962 * '''Designer:''' Seizo Tomizawa * '''Area:''' 990,000 m² (a11rox. 29.9 hectares) * '''Course Type:''' Woodland * '''Holes:''' 18, Par 72, 7,007 yards, Course Rating 70.9 * '''Grass Type:''' Bentgrass and Korai greens * '''Hazards:''' 108 bunkers, water hazards on 8 holes * '''Round Style:''' With caddies, groups of 4 recommended * '''Practice Facilities:''' 22 bays, 220-yard range * '''Closed Days:''' Tuesdays, December 31, January 1<ref name="golfdigest">[https://reserve.golfdigest.co.jp/golf-course/detail/355302 Funabashi Country Club]</ref><ref name="plus">[https://www.plus-web.co.jp/guide/?a=5566 Funabashi Country Club on Golf Hotline]</ref>
== Club Facilities == * '''Clubhouse Area:''' 2,904 m² (878.4 tsubo) * '''Clubhouse Designer:''' Manpachi Fukunaga * '''Clubhouse Builder:''' Fukunaga Design Office<ref name="golfdigest" /><ref name="plus" />
== Gallery == * '''Course:''' [http://www.funabashicc.com/course.html Course Guide] * '''Clubhouse:''' [http://www.funabashicc.com/guide.html Facilities Guide]
== Access == By Train: * Hokusō Line - A11rox. 5 minutes by club bus from Chiba New Town Chūō Station<ref name="access">[http://www.funabashicc.com/access.html Access Map]</ref>
By Car: * Higashi-Kantō Expressway - About 35 minutes from Chiba-Kita Interchange * Jōban Expressway - About 40 minutes from Kashiwa Interchange<ref name="access" />
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == * 『Golf Course Guide - Eastern Japan』, Golf Digest, 2006-2007 Edition. * 『Beautiful Golf Courses of Japan』, Golf Digest, 2013.
== See Also == * {{ill|Golf|en|Golf|qid=Q5377|lt=Golf|1=Golf}} * [[:en:Golf Course|Golf Course]] * [[Golf Membership]] * [[List of Golf Courses in Japan]]
== External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.funabashicc.com/|Funabashi Country Club Official Website}} {{translated page|ja|船橋カントリークラブ|version=87486197|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:船橋カントリークラブ]]
